title: Your Data, Their Algorithm

modules.title: The App That Knows Too Much
modules.sub: Discovering how an app seems to read your mind
modules.description: Learners follow a character who starts noticing suspiciously accurate recommendations and wonders how the app figured them out so quickly. They explore the opening mystery and make their first guesses about what data could have been collected and from where.

modules.title: What Gets Collected Without Asking
modules.sub: Uncovering the data trails you leave behind every day
modules.description: Learners investigate the types of data apps and devices quietly gather — from location pings and scroll habits to microphone access and purchase history. They review real-style app permission screens and decide which data grabs feel surprising or unfair.

modules.title: How AI Builds a Profile of You
modules.sub: Seeing how data points combine into a detailed picture
modules.description: Learners discover how AI systems stitch together individual data points to make inferences about personality, interests, mood, and behaviour. They interact with a simplified profile-builder to see how quickly a few data inputs can produce eerily accurate predictions about a person.

modules.title: The Trade-Off: Convenience vs. Privacy
modules.sub: Weighing what you gain against what you give up
modules.description: Learners face a series of realistic scenarios where a useful feature comes at the cost of sharing more personal data, and they must decide whether the trade feels worth it. They reflect on who benefits most from these exchanges and whether the deal is ever truly equal.

modules.title: Choose What You Share — and How
modules.sub: Taking back control of your own digital footprint
modules.description: Learners walk through actual privacy settings, permission toggles, and data-sharing options found in common apps and platforms. They practise adjusting settings to match their own comfort level and learn practical strategies for limiting data collection without losing access to things they value.

modules.title: Write Your Own Digital Privacy Rules
modules.sub: Creating personal guidelines for how you share data online
modules.description: Learners draft their own digital privacy rulebook — a short, personalised set of principles that captures what they will and won't share, and why. They finish the course by revisiting the opening character's story and applying their new rules to advise on what that character should do differently.

synopsis: Follow a character who keeps getting eerily accurate recommendations and targeted messages — and has to piece together exactly how an AI learned so much about them so fast. You'll make real decisions about data permissions, app settings, and what your digital footprint actually looks like.
rationale: Teenagers are among the heaviest users of AI-powered platforms yet receive almost no structured guidance on how their data fuels those systems or what rights and choices they actually have.
learning_outcome: Students can identify what personal data AI systems collect, explain how that data is used to build behavioral profiles, and make informed decisions about their own privacy settings and sharing habits.
source_signals: Tali Horowitz: The Key Issue with AI Chatbots and Teens
Tali Horowitz: How Teens Are Really Using AI
Digital Citizenship: Teaching Teens to Navigate the online World Responsibly | BWWT Ep. 240
The Risks of AI in Schools Outweigh the Benefits, Report Says
